content: Remote OK Museum Specialist Smithsonian Institution Duties<br><br>The Museum Specialist (Offsite) supports NMAHâs offsite collections program. This is a core operational function supporting preservation, accountability, and access to the museumâs collections.<br><br><strong>In This Position, You Will<br><br></strong><ul><li>Select appropriate space and apply appropriate methods, materials and equipment, to move, house, and store objects.</li><li>Reorganize collections as needed to optimize space utilization in accordance with established collections management procedures and priorities. </li><li>Oversee object preparation for shipments, including the construction of containers and object supports, for safe and secure movement. </li><li>Gather, record, reconcile, and update collections information for the documentation of Offsite collections in manual and automated records.</li><li>Assume responsibility for the security and safety of assigned storage spaces and collections, applying established environmental monitoring procedures and reporting variances.</li><li>Document, preserve, store, and move objects, including objects with hazardous material components, in accordance with established safety and handling procedures. <br><br></li></ul><strong>Requirements<br><br></strong>Conditions of employment<br><br><ul><li>Requires career/career-conditional status or eligibility.</li><li>Males born after 12/31/59 must be registered with Selective Service.<br><br></li></ul><strong>Conditions of Employment<br><br></strong>Qualification requirements, including one year time-in-grade at the next lower grade level for promotion candidates, must be met within 30 days of the job announcement closing date. See the 'Required Documents' section for additional information.<br><br>For information on qualification requirements, see <em>Qualification Standards Handbook for </em><em>General Schedule Positions</em> viewable on<br><br><strong>Qualifications<br><br></strong><strong>Experience: </strong>You qualify for this position if you have one year of specialized experience equivalent to at least the <strong>GS-07</strong> level in the Federal Service. <strong>For this position, specialized experience is defined as providing collections management work for a historical museum or cultural institution including managing and preserving collections; using and maintaining a collection information system or database; and conducting directed research in support of a historical museum or cultural institutionâs collections, exhibitions, and other related projects. <br><br></strong>Your resume must be no more than two (2) pages and should clearly demonstrate how your experience aligns with the responsibilities and specialized experience required for this position. Do not copy language directly from the vacancy announcement, as you will be deemed ineligible for consideration. Instead, provide detailed, descriptive information about your actual experience.<br><br>Experience refers to paid and unpaid experience, including volunteer work done through National Service programs (e.g., Peace Corps, AmeriCorps) and other organizations (e.g., professional; philanthropic; religious; spiritual; community, student, social). Volunteer work helps build critical competencies, knowledge, and skills and can provide valuable training and experience that translates directly to paid employment. You will receive credit for all qualifying experience, including volunteer experience.<br><br>Part-time and/or unpaid experience related to this position will be considered to determine the total number of years and months of experience. Be sure to note the number of paid or unpaid hours worked each week.<br><br><strong>Or Education:</strong> <strong>2 full academic years of progressively higher level graduate education <em>or</em> master's or equivalent graduate degree related to the position.<br><br></strong>Special Instructions for Foreign Education: If you are qualifying by education and/or you have education completed in a foreign college/university described above, it is your responsibility to provide transcripts and proof of U.S. accreditation for foreign study. For instructions on where to fax these documents, see the "Required Documents" section of this announcement.<br><br><strong>Or a Combination:</strong> Education and experience may be combined to meet the basic qualifications; For a full explanation of this option please see the<br><br>In an effort to streamline our hiring process, <strong>SI employees whose current position of record is in the same job series (1016) and who are in a Federal competitive service appointment</strong> as this advertised position, are not required to submit a transcript with your application package. However, if selected, you will be required to provide a copy of your transcript before your start date in the position.<br><br>Time-in-Grade Requirements: Under competitive merit promotion procedures, any individual who is currently holding, or who has held within the previous 52 weeks, a General Schedule position under a non-temporary appointment in the competitive or excepted service, must meet "time-in-grade" requirements (have served 52 weeks at the next lower grade of the grade for which you are applying).<br><br>Education<br><br>This job does not have an education qualification requirement.<br><br>Additional information<br><br><ul><li>This position is included in the bargaining unit. <br><br></li></ul><strong>Recruitment Incentive:</strong> Recruitment incentive(s) may be authorized for this position. However, approval for incentives are contingent upon various availability. If authorized, certain incentives will require the incumbent to sign a service agreement to remain in the Federal government for a certain time period.<br><br><strong>Note:</strong> This statement does not imply nor guarantee an incentive will be offered and paid.<br><br><strong>Selections may be made for vacancies across the Smithsonian Institution (SI). By applying to this position, you agree to allow your application and other personal information to be shared with one or more SI hiring officials for employment consideration for same/similar positions within the location(s) identified in this announcement.<br><br></strong>Candidates should be committed to improving the efficiency of the Federal government, passionate about the ideals of our American republic, and committed to upholding the rule of law and the United States Constitution.<br><br>The Smithsonian offers a comprehensive benefits package that includes, in part, vacation and sick leave, holidays, health/life insurance, accident insurance, and excellent retirement program options.<br><br>Please see Benefits at<br><br>Flexible Spending Accounts -<br><br>Health Insurance -<br><br>Leave -<br><br>Life Insurance -<br><br>Long Term Care Insurance -<br><br>Retirement Program - exec, design, amazon Suitland, 2026-07-16T16:42:30+00:00 1134957 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-museum-specialist-smithsonian-institution-1134957 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png Date Posted OptN Inc <ul><li>Home</li><li>Careers&lt
